0: Oh, you're back. Our guests can't seem to get enough of this kinderpunsch─I'm near rushed off my feet. You wouldn't fancy giving me a hand, would you?
Ah, you're back. The people can't get enough of our punch, and it's all I can do to keep mugs filled. I could use your help with the serving again, if you are willing?

1: We had a lady stop by earlier who insisted we take a whole bunch of strange ingredients, saying something about the spirit of culinary adventure? Still, some people seem to enjoy adding them to the punch as an unusual twist.

I should mention that a curious lady came by earlier. She spoke passionately about the “spirit of culinary adventure” before gifting us an array of unusual ingredients. Though I was skeptical at first, quite a few guests have enjoyed them in their beverage as an accent.

2: If you want to throw a little something extra in when you serve up a mug, I certainly won't say anything. I'll just be happy for the break!
So if you have the urge add a little something extra when you serve up a mug, I leave it at your discretion. I would just be glad for the break!

4: You bring the pans to a gentle simmer.The enticing scent of mulled punch draws someone over to the stall!
You bring the pots of punch to a gentle simmer. The enticing aroma attracts someone to the stall!

6: The owner of the Carline Canopy.

7: The proprietor of the Drowned Wench.

10: Lord commander of the Temple Knights.
The lord commander of the Temple Knights.

11: A representative of the East Aldenard Trading Company.

12: Satrap from the Near East and diminutive dragon.
The satrap of Radz-at-Han and a diminutive dragon.

13: World explorer in search of the unknown.
A world explorer in search of the unknown.

14: Something smells quite intriguing over here! I already sampled the new flavors of punch earlier, so… Could I have a mug of Gridanian kinderpunsch? Let's see how yours compares to the Carline Canopy house blend!

I thought I smelled a lovely aroma! I've already sampled the new varieties, so may I please have a mug of Gridanian punch? It will be interesting to see how yours compares to the Canopy's house blend.

18: That's a fine blend. Thank you─you've been such a help.
That's very tasty indeed! Thank you─you've been a great help.

19: The Starlight Stalls are getting quite the reputation, and we've attracted a lot of visitors from outside of Gridania this Starlight.
The Starlight Stalls have earned quite a stellar reputation─pardon the pun─and there are noticeably more guests from outside Gridania this year.

20: And when I look around, all I see are smiling faces─Gridanians, visitors, children, grown ups…Which, of course, is great news for us stallholders.
From locals to visitors and children to grown-ups, the joy is plain to see on every face. Nothing could bring us stallholders more joy.

21: This is…grated chanterelle? Interesting. These mushrooms have a rich, distinctive aroma that makes them highly valued in cuisine or as medicine─but it's quite surprising to come across in a sweet drink.
Is this grated chanterelle? Interesting. These fragrant mushrooms are sought after for culinary and medicinal purposes, but I'd never imagined tasting it in a sweet drink.

22: Though the flavor certainly requires a mature palate, it gives a certain depth. Yes, I'd say it works.
While it might require a more mature palate, there's a depth to this punch that's quite enjoyable. I'd be happy to have it again.

23: Right, I'd best be heading back.Feel free to stop by any time, okay? You're always welcome at the Canopy.
Well, I should return to my duties. When you have a spare moment from your own, do come and relax at the Canopy.

24: Well, if it ain't <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! Take no mind o' ol' Baderon 'ere─jus' stoppin' by fer a look at these Limsa mugs I've been 'earing so much about. Don't s'pose I could trouble ye for a mugful?
I couldn't 'elp but come to see our Lominsan mugs bein' used. An' suffice it to say I'd be much obliged fer some punch too.

28: Aye, the sour citrus an' the sting o' the spice makes fer a right fine tipple.
Aye, the sourness o' the citrus an' the sting o' the spice make fer a right fine drop.

29: Fer most Lominsans, the cold in Gridania this time o' year is more bitin' than bracin'─but a nice warm drink makes all the difference. This should keep me goin' as I look round the other stalls.

We Lominsans ain't so keen on Gridania this time o' year on account o' the freezin' cold, but a nice warm drink makes a world o' difference. This'll keep me goin' as I take in the sights 'neath the snow.

30: Bugger me, that's sweeter than a siren's kiss in a sea storm! Chocolate…an' an 'ole sack o' sugar too, by my reckonin'.
Bugger me, that's sweeter than a siren's kiss! Ye put chocolate in 'ere, did ye? An' a whole bleedin' sack o' sugar too, by the taste of it.

31: Still, there's folk what prefer a more…unusual tipple. An' ye'd be surprised at 'ow many seadogs 'ave a sweet tooth, what with sugar bein' so important for preservin' food on long voyages. Aye, ye might be onto somethin' 'ere.

Still, there's folk as like somethin' a little unusual. An' ye'd be surprised 'ow many seadogs 'ave a sweet tooth, what with sugar bein' used to preserve food on voyages. Aye, I reckon ye might be onto somethin' 'ere.

32: Right, I'd best be gettin' on. I thank ye fer the 'ospitality─next time ye find yerself in Limsa, stop by the Wench and I'll gladly return the gesture.
Anyroad, I'd best be on me way. Thanks fer the 'ospitality─next time yer in Limsa, stop by the Wench and I'll return the gesture.

33: Miounne invited me up to Gridania for Starlight─so I had to stop by and see your face, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! Couldn't trouble you for a mug of Ul'dahn mulled punch, could I?
Miounne invited me to Gridania for the Starlight Celebration, and I couldn't say no. Might I trouble you for a mug of Ul'dahn punch?

37: Now that hits the spot! Such a full-bodied flavor—I can tell you didn't skimp on the butter.
Oh my, what a lovely, full-bodied brew! You definitely didn't skimp on the butter.

38: You've got a surefire hit on your hands here. All it needs is a good plate of crumpets on the side, and people in Ul'dah would come flocking. How would you feel about brewing a pot or two in the Quicksand kitchen for me some time, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>?
It'd go great with crumpets and would definitely be a hit in Ul'dah. What do you say you come and brew it for me at the Quicksand sometime?

39: Hehe, I'm just pullin' your leg! Mayhap it's what comes from havin' spent so long as the one servin' the tea—you can take the girl out of the Quicksand, but you can't take the Quicksand out of the girl!
Hehe, I'm just kiddin'. Long as I've been in the business of servin' food and drink, it's a force of habit to consider the possibilities.

40: Oh, you've outdone yourself! This is cactus, unless I'm mistaken? Though I believe it's more often called “nopal” when used as an ingredient.
Well now, what a delicious drink! You've added nopal─cactus flesh─unless I'm mistaken?

41: Plenty of folk enjoy roasted nopal in savory dishes, but I wouldn't have expected it to work so well in somethin' sweet. Might be a good addition to the Quicksand's menu!
Plenty of folk enjoy nopal roasted in savory dishes, but it works surprisingly well with sweet flavors too. I might have to try adding it to our menu...

42: Thank you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>. I'll be headin' off now, but don't be a stranger—stop by the Quicksand now and then, you hear?
Thank you kindly! Whenever you're in Ul'dah, be sure to stop by at the Quicksand, you hear?

43: Mulled punch for the festivalgoers? What a delightful idea!
Mulled punch for the guests? What a delightful idea.

44: As always, my dearest sister, Kan-E, has taken far too much upon herself—and so, I insisted that she allow me to take charge of the Seedseer visit to the festivities here at the Starlight Stalls. Might you be kind enough to furnish me with a mug of Gridanian mulled punch?
Lest you wonder, my sister Kan-E is busy as is her wont, so I am attending the festivities in her stead. I shall have a Gridanian punch, if you would be so kind.

48: How fragrant and delicate! I daresay none shall take umbrage at it bearing Gridania's name, whether they be guest or lifelong resident.
How fragrant and delicious! 'Tis a flavor that we can proudly call Gridanian to forestborn and visitors alike.

49: Might I trouble you for another two mugfuls, for my dear brother and sister?
Might I ask for two more mugs of the same, for my brother and sister?

50: You have my thanks. My floating, furry companions and I will see this punch delivered ere it grows cold—I leave matters here in your eminently capable hands!
Wonderful, I shall have my attendants deliver these ere they grow cold. My gratitude for the drinks─and for your contribution to the celebration.

52: …by the Twelve, you gave me a fright! It's just a sweet confection made to <Emphasis>resemble</Emphasis> a pom, you say? For the sake of my two trembling companions, I'd best make certain!
...So it is simply a confection made to resemble a pom? That is certainly a relief─and for my companions especially. Without further ado, then, I shall give it a taste.

53: Well, it's a relief to find it perfectly pleasant—delicious, really—and that no moogles were harmed in the process. Just…try not to traumatize too many children, won't you?
Well now, that is perfectly delicious! Just...try not to frighten the children overmuch, do you hear?

58: Ahh, how exquisitely the rich cinnamon balances the saccharinity. 'Tis a moment of sweet respite in a mug.
Ahh, how exquisitely the cinnamon punctuates the sweetness. 'Tis the very taste of respite.

59: Full glad am I to see the spirit and warmth of the Starlight Celebration thus spread throughout Eorzea. While I take pride that the tradition originates from our fair nation of Ishgard, I am indebted to the celebrants for their diligent work in seeing it upheld.
The Starlight Celebration has its roots in Ishgard. To see the warmth and cheer being spread in all corners of Eorzea makes me feel both proud and grateful.

60: This aroma…! The mulled punch may be quintessentially Gridanian, but I detect the unmistakable influence of Ishargardian flavors. An intriguing concept—and most appropriate, given that Ishgard is the birthplace of Starlight.
Well, now... While this punch tastes quintessentially Gridanian, there is no mistaking the Ishgardian accent. Might this be an acknowledgement of the Starlight Celebration's origins...?

61: …Simply exquisite. <sigh> Forgive me—such a generous helping of birch syrup is an indulgence I allow myself only on special occasions, and today I find it all the sweeter.
Yet regardless of the intent, the generous amount of birch syrup is a wonderful touch, and during this most special of seasons, I shall allow myself the indulgence.

62: A drink shared with a friend is a fine gift indeed. Now, I must return to my duties—there are many children yet awaiting their own Starlight gift, and 'twould not do to keep them overlong!
My thanks for the fine drink and company both. By your leave, I shall return to my duties—many children yet await their Starlight gifts, and 'twould not do to keep them!

63: Well, well, if it isn't <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! Having concluded some discussions with valued business partners in the vicinity, I thought to drop by the festivities—but what an unexpected surprise and delight to find your esteemed personage manning a punch stand.

Oh my, if it isn't <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>! Having concluded some business discussions nearby, I thought to drop by the festivities. What a pleasant surprise to find your esteemed self manning a stand.

64: Needless to say, it would be most remiss of me to turn down such a kind gesture—and to leave without sampling the local wares. Would you be so kind as to procure me a mug of your Ul'dahn mulled punch?
You are serving punch to guests, it would seem, and far be it from me to turn it down. I should like a mug of the Ul'dahn variety, if you'd be so kind.

68: That the taste of Ul'dah should elicit such distant nostalgia! I have spent many years steeped in Hingashi's unique and inimitable “omotenashi”—roughly translated, the word refers to the deep-seated culture of wholeheartedly caring for and serving one's guests, though I fear the common tongue too impoverished to encapsulate the nuance correctly. And yet, there does indeed remain a certain warmth and charm to the hospitality of one's homeland…

Ah, what an unmistakably Ul'dahn─and nostalgic─taste! And what a fine example this stall is of “omotenashi,” which, in Hingan, refers to the deep-seated practice of wholeheartedly caring for one's guests.

69: And I cannot help but note the selection of three different flavors, no doubt to encourage repeat purchases. Allow me to express my admiration for the business acumen of the stall operator!
I am also impressed with the offering of three flavors─a wise business decision that encourages repeated patronage.

70: Indeed, I must say the entire enterprise is quite inspired. A market held only during the Starlight season, and offering children the opportunity to encounter myriad peoples and cultures—as a representative of the East Aldenard Trading Company, I shall be watching future developments with great interest.

Indeed, the entire enterprise of the Starlight Stalls is inspired. And in offering the chance for children to experience other cultures, it has attracted my interest─as an individual and a representative as the East Aldenard Trading Company both!

71: I beg your pardon, did you say…namazu whisker? Come now, surely you jest—I presume it to be some novelty, say, a strand of spice carefully arrayed to appear as though an errant whisker, floating upon the punch?
I beg your pardon, did you say “Namazu whisker”? Come now, that is surely a jest. I assume you are referring to some manner of ingredient that appears as a whisker—a strand of spice, mayhap.

73: Ahem! Merchants must view innovation as opportunity—and one certainly cannot deny the value of an entirely…<Emphasis>unique</Emphasis> experience.
When seeking to introduce a new specialty, I suppose uniqueness is a way to pierce a crowded market...

74: Now, if you will excuse me, I shall continue to peruse the stalls and their wares. I find myself quite invigorated after the warming drink!
Well, the drink has had quite an invigorating effect. So if you will excuse me, I shall continue to peruse the stalls.

75: What fortune to run into you here, my friend. I heard tell of the festivities when attending to some matters beyond Radz-at-Han, and thought to take a brief detour around the stalls.
What fortune to meet you here, my friend. I had heard tell of the festivities while attending to some matters abroad, and decided to make a detour and tour the stalls.

76: A familiar aroma drew me over, and it would be most improper to depart empty-handed in the face of such hospitality. May I trouble you for a mug of mulled punch?
I must say, I also smell some familiar fragrances... If you are serving beverages to guests, then 'twould be improper not to accept. May I trouble you for a mug?

80: I see… These spices are from Radz-at-Han. It brings me great joy to know that the produce of our humble nation is thus embraced by other cultures.
Ah, so you have used Hannish spices. It never fails to gladden me to see our produce being embraced in foreign lands.

81: Though I may be foreign to this land, I recognise well the smiling faces around me. The joy people find in festivals is the same, wheresoever they call home.
And to see that, no matter where we hail from, we are united in the joy we find in festivals.

82: Perhaps there is a way in which we might incorporate some aspects of Eorzean culture into our festivals in Radz-at-Han one day. Ever am I keen to embrace the ways of others—especially should it serve as a boon to my people.
Perhaps I shall try to incorporate some aspects of Eorzean celebrations into our own. Yes, I believe it would make a fine gift to my people.

83: This is quite a spice-laden punch you have prepared, my friend. Yes, to the extent that even this vessel may appreciate the flavor.
<chuckle> I see you have spared no spice in this drink, my friend. I was able to taste it even with this vessel.

84: …Would it have been to Zero's liking, I wonder? Her special curry has quite the reputation, following her visit to Mehryde's Meyhane. I hear they still make a batch every now and then—which always results in a lively evening.
...I daresay Zero would enjoy it. They still serve her special curry at the meyhane on occasion. And when they do, it never fails to get lively.

85: Yet I imagine the children of this land have not the same tolerance for spice as those in Radz-at-Han, though they seem to boast the same curiosity. Pray take care, lest it prove too much for them!

But to return to the subject of the drink─do take care with the amount of spice. I expect the children here are no less inquisitive than those in Radz-at-Han, and may unknowingly bite off more than they can chew!

86: …How is it that I always end up running into you—serving punch at a festival, of all places? I only stopped by since an urgent request brought me to the area. Well, since I'm here, I suppose a drink wouldn't hurt… One Ul'dahn mulled punch, please.

I happened to have an urgent request nearby, so I thought I'd visit the stalls...and who should I run into again but you. Well, seeing you serving punch is a new experience at least. I'll have an Ul'dahn one please.

90: Not bad. While the native flora in Thanalan bears many similarities to that of Yyasulani, the cuisine has evolved along quite disparate lines in each region.
Not bad, not bad. It's interesting to note that while the flora of Thanalan and Yyasulani bear many similarities, their cuisines have evolved rather differently.

91: When my work as a gleaner took me around the world, it was rare that I could spare the time for a proper sit-down meal. Perhaps my future travels will allow me to enjoy the flavors of my destinations, not only the sights and sounds.

Though my work for the guildship took me to all manner of places, I rarely had time to enjoy proper meals. As an independent contractor, I can allow myself to take in not only the sights at my destinations, but the tastes as well.

92: My thanks for the drink. I think I'll look around the rest of the Starlight Stalls, before taking a leisurely stroll through the Black Shroud. There is much to explore!
My thanks for the drink. I think I'll take a look around the Starlight Stalls and then go on a stroll through the forest. There is much to see and do!

93: …Tell me this isn't what I think it is.

95: Not you, too…I will concede, they are not entirely inedible, but I…question their suitability as an accompaniment to a sweet drink.

Not you too, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>... They aren't entirely inedible, I concede, but putting them in a sweet drink? There are <Emphasis>limits</Emphasis>, for gods' sake.

96: <sigh> My thanks for the drink. Traveling the world opens your eyes to all kinds of things—and people.
<sigh> When you travel the world, you truly encounter all kinds of things─and people. I suppose I owe you for that at least...
